title: stellaria graminea lusus brevifolia
intro: Stellaria graminea lusus brevifolia, also known as the lesser celandine or pygmy stitchwort, is a perennial plant species in the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of saponins and flavonoids. The plant has various health benefits, including the ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Stellaria graminea lusus brevifolia can be prepared as an infusion or decoction for its herbal uses, with the root and leaves being used in traditional medicine.
